name: Focaccia
recipeIngredient: 2/3 fresh cake yeast, dissolved in 2/3 cup warm water
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix 2 cups flour with the dissolved yeast.
2. Knead the dough for 10 minutes, either by hand or in the bowl of an electric mixer with a dough hook.
3. Shape the dough into a ball and place it in an oiled bowl to rise until doubled in bulk, about 3 hours.
4. Punch down the dough, turn it out onto a floured board, and knead again, incorporating the remaining flour, 1/3 cup olive oil, water, and salt.
5. Knead until the dough is smooth and elastic. Let it rise again in a covered bowl for another 3 hours.
6. Preheat the oven to 400°F (200°C).
7. Divide the dough into two portions and roll out on a well-floured board to circles or squares that are about 1/2 inch thick.
8. Brush the dough with the remaining olive oil and press toppings of your choice onto each circle.
9. Bake on a baking sheet until golden brown, about 20 minutes. Serve warm or at room temperature, cut into wedges, squares, or triangles.
Toppings:
Thinly sliced rounds of yellow onion
Paper-thin crosswise slices of tomatoes and chunks of sun-dried tomatoes
Oregano leaves and slivers of garlic
Coarse salt and olive oil
Rosemary sprigs, coarse salt, and olive oil
Black olive halves and coarse salt
Sage leaves, coarse salt, and olive oil
